reason: ext4_truncate releases i_data_sem with up_write at line 4150 before the err check at line 4151, and the out_stop path also stops the journal. The reported missing cleanup is already present on this path.
At line 601 ext4_bread returned an ERR_PTR. No buffer_head reference was acquired, and out_free starts by releasing only previously completed iterations.
evidence: IS_ERR result is not a releasable buffer_head

reason: A valid buffer_head is acquired at line 600, the bad-magic branch jumps to out_free, and the cleanup loop starts with i--. The current buffer_head is therefore not released.
A valid buffer_head is still stored for the current index when checksum verification fails at line 615. The shared out_free loop skips that current index, leaking its reference.
ret == 0 from ext4_map_blocks means the logical range is not mapped and enters the normal insertion branch. It is not an error condition; only later failures in that branch go to out.
Failure of ext4_fc_record_modified_inode is assigned to ret and jumps to out, whose unconditional return 0 hides the error.
A negative ext4_map_blocks result jumps to the shared out label, which returns 0 instead of propagating ret.
Failure of ext4_ext_remove_space jumps to out after metadata state may have been changed, and the shared return 0 swallows the failure.
ext4_fc_record_modified_inode failure jumps to out and is converted into a successful return 0.
ext4_get_fc_inode_loc failure jumps to out and the function returns 0. The failure is not propagated to the replay caller.
ext4_handle_dirty_metadata failure jumps to out and the shared return 0 hides the metadata write error.
ext4_mark_inode_used failure jumps to out and is followed by unconditional return 0, so the replay reports success after the failure.
This is the same ext4_truncate error path as sample 001. up_write(&ei->i_data_sem) executes before the error branch, and out_stop performs journal cleanup.
ext4_ext_insert_extent failure jumps to out, which calls iput and returns 0 instead of propagating ret.
ext4_ext_replay_update_ex failure jumps to out, whose unconditional return 0 swallows the error.

When the per-group allocation fails, the exit path frees the newly allocated meta_group_info table for the first group. For later groups the existing table is retained intentionally; no newly acquired object is left unowned by this branch.
At line 154 bh is NULL, so no buffer_head reference exists to release. The function intentionally converts a directory hole into -EFSCORRUPTED.
The line 601 branch is an ERR_PTR result from ext4_bread, not a successfully acquired buffer_head. Releasing it would be invalid; only prior successful iterations are released.
The IS_ERR and NULL branches at line 95 represent failed or absent acquisition. The acquired buffer_head is released on the !ext4_buffer_uptodate path and transferred to delayed_call on success.
fiemap_fill_next_extent returning 1 means the userspace extent buffer is full. The function intentionally returns success after reporting the available extents.
ret is a local selection flag. A nonzero value means no suitable Orlov group was found and deliberately falls back to the alternative allocation policy.
evidence: fallback label is the intended control-flow path

reason: ext4_get_inode_loc failure causes this query helper to return size 0, which is the documented inability-to-use-inline-space result; no acquired iloc buffer exists on the error branch.
ext4_da_write_inline_data_begin returning 1 means the inline write was handled. Returning 0 is the success convention, not swallowed error handling.
A positive mapped result from ext4_map_blocks takes the normal fast path to out and returns a successfully populated iomap. The condition is not an error.
ext4_try_to_write_inline_data returning 1 indicates the inline write completed and ext4_write_begin returns 0 by design.
ext4_mb_seq_groups_show is a seq_file diagnostic reader. On buddy-load failure it prints an I/O error marker and returns 0 to complete the read protocol intentionally.
ext4_fc_reserve_space returns a pointer and uses NULL as its documented allocation-failure result. The caller-facing API does not promise propagation of the internal integer ret.
ext4_get_inode_loc fails before iloc.bh is acquired, and returning NULL with *retval set is the function's error contract.
ext4_fname_setup_filename returning -ENOENT means the lookup has no matching entry; returning NULL is the normal not-found result and the filename object was not initialized for cleanup.
ext4_fname_prepare_lookup returning -ENOENT means lookup-not-found. The function intentionally returns NULL; there is no acquired resource on this branch.
